summary: Iteration 18 - Full regression of notification fan-out + /api/activity + auth refresh + Activity Feed UI. Backend: 14/14 pytest cases PASSED covering status-change, comment, attachment, demo create/update/complete, followup create/complete fan-out (each with actor-exclusion + owner + manager assertions), /api/activity manager-vs-counselor scoping, actor_id filter, event_type filter, days filter, and the silent-refresh auth flow (drop access_token cookie -> /auth/me 401 -> /auth/refresh 200 + new cookie -> /auth/me 200). Frontend: Activity Feed page renders with all expected testids (activity-feed-page, activity-refresh-btn, activity-search-input, activity-type-filter, activity-range-filter, activity-actor-filter) and 73 activity items grouped under date headings; nav-activity-feed link present; bell dropdown (notif-panel + mark-all-read-btn testids) now opens correctly and displays the newly created fan-out notifications (demo_scheduled, demo_updated, demo_completed, followup_added, followup_completed) for the manager. Clicking an activity card opens the lead detail sheet on /leads (URL is intentionally cleaned by LeadsPage after opening - confirmed in code).

issues: Recharts containers continue to spam width(-1)/height(-1) warnings on initial render (cosmetic, carried over from iteration 17).
Multiple 404s on static resource fetches during page load (carried over from iteration 17). Not blocking but noisy in console.

action_items: Cosmetic: silence Recharts width/height warnings by giving each ResponsiveContainer a fixed aspect ratio or explicit min-h/min-w.
Cosmetic: investigate the 3-6 404 resource fetches on dashboard / activity pages and either remove the requests or add the missing assets/icons.

(Optional UX) Activity item naming: bell dropdown notification rows use data-testid=`notif-${id}` while activity items use `activity-item-${id}`. Consider unifying the prefix (e.g. `notif-item-${id}`) for easier future automation. Not a defect.
critical_code_review_comments: notify_lead_stakeholders() in server.py:303 is well-defensive (try/except around every awaitable) and correctly excludes the actor via recipients.discard(str(actor_id)). The fan-out cleanly persists into the notifications collection AND fires web push via send_web_push() with no risk of breaking the parent endpoint on failure - good pattern.

/api/activity (server.py:3671) correctly scopes counselors/telecallers to lead.assigned_to == self via a pre-resolved lead-id $in query, avoiding any leak. Managers/admins get the full org. Filter intersection logic for lead_id + role-scoping is correct.

server.py is now 8059 lines (was 7787 last iteration) - still well over the 700-line guideline. Recommend splitting into routers/* modules (auth, leads, demos, followups, notifications, activity, push).

LeadsPage.jsx line 182 clears the URL after auto-opening the sheet (window.history.replaceState({}, '', '/leads')) - intentional, prevents re-open on refresh. Worth a code comment noting the test-only side effect (URL appears as plain /leads after deep-link).

success_rate.frontend: 100% (Activity feed page + all filters + nav link + bell dropdown + sheet auto-open all verified)

test_credentials: Used orgadmin@demo.com / Demo@123 (actor), manager@demo.com / Demo@123, counselor@demo.com / Demo@123 (owner) - all from /app/memory/test_credentials.md
seed_data_creation: Per test run creates one TEST_FANOUT_<hex> lead assigned to counselor Priya Sharma, one demo on it (scheduled 2026-12-31 10:00 Online), one followup on it (2026-12-30 11:00). Lead is transitioned New -> Contacted. All data prefixed with TEST_FANOUT for easy cleanup. Not auto-cleaned.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest file at /app/backend/tests/test_iteration18_notification_fanout.py uses requests.Session() per role for cookie-based auth. Key payload notes: POST /api/leads requires `lead_source` (NOT `source`) and `course_interested` (NOT `course_interest`); POST /api/leads/{id}/comments requires `note` (NOT `text`); demo create returns `_id` not `id` (test fixture normalises both). The bell-dropdown notification rows use data-testid=`notif-${_id}` (NOT `notif-item-${id}`) - if writing playwright selectors. Activity feed items use data-testid=`activity-item-${id}`. After clicking an activity card the URL becomes `/leads` (no openLead query param) because LeadsPage.jsx:182 strips it via replaceState; the sheet still opens correctly.
rca of the issue: No regressions found. Manager screenshot of /dashboard confirms the bell shows all 5 freshly-emitted fan-out notifications (followup completed, followup scheduled, demo marked interested, demo updated, demo scheduled) - actor exclusion + owner + manager fan-out is working end-to-end on real production traffic. The iteration-17 bell dropdown bug is FIXED (notif-panel + mark-all-read-btn testids now render, popover opens on click).
